info.description: REST Services for IP Classification modeler (dslib).
info.title: IP Classification Web Services

get.summary: Gets an indexed search result of Libraries
get.description: Gets an indexed search result of the Library objects with basic (dslib:SimpleMask) or additional information (dslib:LibraryDetailsMask) as per the mask used.

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: $searchStr
parameters.description: A search text to find one or more items.

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dslib:SimpleMask

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

get.description:
Gets any of the following information of the given Library as per the mask used.
1 - The basic (dslib:SimpleMask) or additional information (dslib:LibraryDetailsMask) of given Library.
2 - Only those subclasses to which the user has Classify Access (dslib:ExpandClassifiableClassesMask).
3 - the basic (dslib:ExpandClassesMask) or additional information (dslib:ExpandClassesDetailsMask) of the subclasses under the given Library.

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: dslib:Library object ID

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dslib:SimpleMask

parameters.name: $depth
parameters.description: Applicable only while using the masks - dslib:ExpandClassesMask or dslib:ExpandClassesDetailsMask or dslib:ExpandClassifiableClassesMask. It is the number of levels to expand under the given Library. Default value is 5. Use a non-zero positive integer. Maximum allowed value is 20. Default value 5 will be used if more than 20 is provided. NOT recommended for taxonomies with more than 50 items.

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

get.summary: Gets an indexed search result of Classes
get.description: Gets an indexed search result of the Classes. By default, returns a total of up to 50 items, can be optionally increased up to 1000 items using $top query parameter. Recommendation: Use $searchStr query parameter for better performances

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: $searchStr
parameters.description: A search text to find one or more items.

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

get.description:
Gets any of the following information based on the mask used.
1 - Class and it's parent (dslib:ClassMask).
2 - Class and it's parent with additional information (dslib:ClassDetailsMask).
3 - Only those subclasses to which the user has Classify Access (dslib:ExpandClassifiableClassesMask).
4 - Subclasses under the given Class with basic (dslib:ExpandClassesMask) or additional information (dslib:ExpandClassesDetailsMask).
5 - Class Attributes of the given Class (dslib:ClassAttributesMask).
6 - Classified Items and Categorization Classified Items in the given Class (dslib:ClassifiedItemsMask).

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: dslib:Class object ID

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dslib:ClassMask

parameters.name: $depth
parameters.description: Applicable only while using the masks - dslib:ExpandClassesMask or dslib:ExpandClassesDetailsMask or dslib:ExpandClassifiableClassesMask. It is the number of levels to expand under the given Class. Default value is 5. Use a non-zero positive integer. Maximum allowed value is 20. Default value 5 will be used if more than 20 is provided. NOT recommended for taxonomies with more than 50 items.

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

post.summary: Fetches the classified items in a Class by filtering on the given search query having classification attributes and values
post.description: Fetches the filtered list of classified items under the taxonomy of given Class by performing index search on the given query (in UQL format) which has classification attributes (of the given class) and values. For more details on query formation, refer the technical article section: "About IP Classification Web Services"

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: dslib:Class object ID

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value of 1000.

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

403.description: FORBIDDEN. The server understood the request, but is refusing to fulfill it. Authorization will not help and the request SHOULD NOT be repeated. If the request method was not HEAD and the server wishes to make public why the request has not been fulfilled, it SHOULD describe the reason for the refusal in the entity. If the server does not wish to make this information available to the client, the status code 404 (Not Found) can be used instead.

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

get.summary: Gets a given information of object classified under Definition class.
get.description: Gets either the 'classification attributes (dslib:ClassificationAttributesMask)' or the 'reverse classification path (dslib:ReverseClassificationMask)' of the given Classified Item object depending on the mask used.

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: dslib:ClassifiedItem object ID

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dslib:ClassifiedItemBaseMask

parameters.name: $classId
parameters.description: Applicable only when using the mask - dslib:ClassificationAttributesMask. To fetch the classification attributes (associated with a particular class in which this classified item is classified) along with their values, send the id of the required class in this parameter

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN

post.summary: Classifies the list of external objects (public http resources) in a given Definition Class.
post.description: Classifies multiple external objects (public http resources) in a given Definition Class and returns the object reference of respective Classified Item or failure message for each object. Maximum 50 objects are only allowed in a service call.

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

StatusOfObjects.description: Status details for each object sent to be classified

154F825612370000D2BEA75EC0CF0800.description: Physical ID of object1 sent to be classifed

ClassifiedItem.description: Object reference of the classified item

154F8256744D000002A6AF5E1AE90900.description: Physical ID of object2 sent to be classifed

Reason.description: Reason for NOT classifying the object (available when failed)

422.description: UNPROCESSABLE ENTITY. All objects in the request body are unclassified. The request was well-formed but could not be processed due to semantic errors or invalid data. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifying the request content.

The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

413.description: PAYLOAD TOO LARGE. The request is larger than the server is willing or able to process. Previously called "Request Entity Too Large".

207.description: MULTI-STATUS. The request was partially successful. Some operations were completed successfully while others failed. The response body contains details about the status of each operation.

classUsage.description: class usage of the parent classification

post.summary: Modifies the classification attributes of the given list of objects classified under categorization class
post.description: Modifies the classification attributes of the given list of objects classified under categorization class. Maximum 50 objects are only allowed in a service call.

parameters.name: SecurityContext
parameters.description: Role.Organization.CollabSpace value Ex:VPLMProjectLeader.MyCompany.Default

parameters.name: ENO_CSRF_TOKEN
parameters.description: csrf token. You can get it from 3DSpace/resources/v1/application/CSRF

parameters.name: $mask
parameters.description: Mask defining what will be returned. Default Mask is:dslib:ClassifiedItemBaseMask

enum: dslib:ClassificationAttributesMask (This mask will return the attributes information with respect to given class in payload)

200.description: OK. The request has succeeded. The information returned with the response is dependent on the method used in the request, for example: GET an entity corresponding to the requested resource is sent in the response; HEAD the entity-header fields corresponding to the requested resource are sent in the response without any message-body; POST an entity describing or containing the result of the action; TRACE an entity containing the request message as received by the end server.

schema.example: Response will be returned with oneOf mask available in the Schema Tab.

400.description: BAD REQUEST. The request could not be understood by the server due to malformed syntax. The client SHOULD NOT repeat the request without modifications.

401.description: UNAUTHORIZED. The request requires user authentication. The response MUST include a WWW-Authenticate header field containing a challenge applicable to the requested resource. The client MAY repeat the request with a suitable Authorization header field. If the request already included Authorization credentials, then the 401 response indicates that authorization has been refused for those credentials. If the 401 response contains the same challenge as the prior response, and the user agent has already attempted authentication at least once, then the user SHOULD be presented the entity that was given in the response, since that entity might include relevant diagnostic information. HTTP access authentication is explained in "HTTP Authentication: Basic and Digest Access Authentication".

500.description: INTERNAL SERVER ERROR. A generic error message, given when an unexpected condition was encountered and no more specific message is suitable.

libraryUsage.description: Library Purpose if parent is Library

classUsage.description: Class Purpose if parent is Class

totalItems.description: total number of the class attributes

name.description: DB name of the class attribute

default.description: default value of the class attribute

description.example: attribute to store the color of the product

multivalued.description: property to decide whether the class attribute allows multiple values or not

authorizedValues.description: The authorized values(range values) of the class attribute

items.description: Authorized value (range value) of the attribute

minimum.description: minimum(inclusive) range value of the class attribute (for integer/real types only)

maximum.description: maximum(inclusive) range value of the class attribute (for integer/real types only)

totalItems.description: total number of the classes in which this item is classified

name.description: DB name of the classification attribute

value.description: The value of the classification attribute (this value would be an array for multivalued attributes)

classUsage.description: Class Purpose if parent is class

libraryUsage.description: library Purpose if parent is library
Library Purpose if parent is library

classUsage.description: Class Purpose if parent is class

Query.description: Search query (in UQL format) with classification attributes (of given class) and values. Enclose the classification attributes in [].
Query.example: [ClassificationAttribute1] : (Blue OR Red OR Green) AND ([ClassificationAttribute2] >= 100 OR [ClassificationAttribute2] < 10) AND NOT [ClassificationAttribute3] <= 2020/11/23

ClassificationAttributes.description: List of classification attributes of the given class which are used in the above query

items.description: classification attribute used in the given query

cestamp.description: latest cestamp value of the classified item object

classId.description: Class Id of class where the Categorization Classified Item is classifed
